original: Major fire at Chartres Cathedral, France, leads to it rebuilt as the high point of French Gothic style
French Jacquerie peasant leader Guillaume Cale captured at the Battle of Mello
Crimean Khan defeats the Great Horde in battle near Worskla, leading to disintegration of the Great Horde
Catholic German monarchy signs League of Neuremberg, in response to the formation of the Protestant Schmalkaldic League
Council of Trent: Paul III sends letters to his bishops, delaying the Council due to war and the difficulty bishops have traveling to Venice
False Dimitri I, an impostor, crowned Russian tsar (rules 1605-1606)
First Dutch settlers arrive from New Jersey to colonize Manhattan Island
Thomas West, Baron de La Mar, is appointed governor of Virginia
Thirty Years' War: Battle of Záblatí, a turning point in the Bohemian Revolt
Netherlands & France sign anti-Spanish Treaty of Compiègne
First American log cabin at Fort Christina (Wilmington, Delaware)
The people of Moscow rise up against Regent Boris Morozov
John Hull opens the first mint in America in Boston
Tornado in Connecticut uproots a 3 ft diameter oak tree
Jacobite Rising: Battle of Glen Shiel - Jacobite army defeated by British forces
Mrs. Clements of England markets the first paste-style mustard
New York passes the first effective law regulating the practice of medicine
Riot breaks out in Boston after customs officials seize sloop owned byJohn Hancockfor alleged customs violation
Burning of British revenue cutter Gaspée by Rhode Islanders
A landslide dam on the Dadu River, caused by an earthquake ten days earlier, collapses and kills 100,000 in Sichuan province, China
First public zoo opens in Paris
Bethel African Methodist Episcopal Church in Philadelphia forms
France's revolutionary regime begins trials
Tripoli declares war on US for refusing tribute
First Barbary War: Tripolitania agrees to peace in war with US over the latter's refusal to make tribute payments; US pays ransom for release of prisoners and lifts naval blockade
First Barbary War: Yussif Karamanli signs a treaty ending hostilities with the United States
First US steamboat to make an ocean voyage leaves NY for Philadelphia
The first Oxford and Cambridge University Boat Race takes place (Oxford wins)
Myall Creek Massacre in Australia: 28 Aboriginal Australians are murdered
Robert Thomson obtains an English patent on a rubber tyre
Chicago Tribune begins publishing
Battle at Vicenza: Austrians beat Sardinia-Piemonte
First telegraph link between NYC and Chicago
German mathematician Bernhard Reiman proposes that space is curved
The first class of the United States Naval Academy students graduate
Britain passes an act putting Canada on the decimal currency system
Battle of Big Bethel (Bethel Church, Great Bethel): Confederate forces repulse a Union attack
Union victory at the Battle of Kellar's Bridge, Kentucky (Licking River)
'Agnes' arrives in New Orleans with 1st ever shipment of frozen beef
Sinmiyangyo: Captain McLane Tilton leads 109 Marines in naval attack on Han River forts on Kanghwa Island, Korea
MLB Boston Red Caps' outfielder Charley Jones becomes 1st to hit 2 HRs in 1 inning, both off of Tom Poorman in a 19-3 win over the visiting Buffalo Bisons
Anti-colonization mass society of Alexandria, Egypt, kills 50 Europeans
Eruption of Tarawera volcano destroys famous pink and white calcium carbonate hot-spring terraces (North Island, New Zealand)
Baltimore catcher Wilbert Robinson sets MLB record by going 7-for-7 in a 9-inning game; Orioles rout St. Louis Cardinals, 25-4 at Oriole Park
Improved Benevolent & Protective Order of Elks forms in Cincinnati
Relief column of 2,000 men set out from Tianjin to relieve foreigners trapped in Peking, China
Patent for window envelope granted to H. F. Callahan
First forest fire lookout tower is put into operation in Greenville, Maine
France and Japan sign an agreement to maintain the independence and integrity of China, equality for all nations in trading with China, and the status quo in the Far East
Aeronautical Society of New York, the first flying club, opens
The Australian Parliament passes the Invalid and Old Age Pensions Act providing for pensions for British subjects (excluding aborigines) at age 65
British and French troops conquer German colony of Cameroon
Hashemite-led Arabs of the Hejaz rise up against the ruling Ottoman Empire in the Great Arab Revolt of World War I
60,000 people of Petrograd Russia welcome Prince Kropotkin (banned 41 years) returning after February Russian Revolution
Limburg mine workers strike in the Netherlands
First political convention broadcast on radio - Republicans in Cleveland
Inaugural service for the United Church of Canada, a union of Presbyterian, Methodist, and Congregationalist churches held in Toronto Arena
MLB Philadelphia Phillies Russ Wrightstone hits for the cycle
Winnipeg Rugby Football Club forms
Norway occupies East Greenland
First demonstration of artificial lightning in Pittsfield, Massachusetts
FIFA World Cup Final, Stadio Nazionale PNF, Rome, Italy: Angelo Schiavio scores the winner in extra time as Italy beats Czechoslovakia, 2-1
USSR & Romania re-establish diplomatic relations
English cricketer Charlie Barnett makes 98 by lunch in England v Australia test match at Trent Bridge (closest any Englishman has come to scoring 100 runs before lunch)
MGM cartoon character Barney Bear debuts
Canada declares war on Italy
French government moves to Bordeaux
German "Dutch" Q-ship Atlantis sinks Norwegian tanker
German 5th Armoured division occupies Rouen
Italy declares war on France and Great Britain as part of its Axis alliance with Nazi Germany
Norway surrenders to Nazi Germany after 62 days of fighting
FDRbecomes 1st US President to visit a foreign country during wartime
Nazi forces carry out a massacre of 642 civilians in the French village Oradour-sur-Glane
Relief pitcher Joe Nuxhall at 15 years, 316 days, debuts for Cincinnati Reds; youngest player in MLB history; becomes All Star and broadcaster
World War II: In Distomo, Boeotia Prefecture, Greece 218 men, women and children are massacred by German troops
US destroyer William D. Porter (Willie Dee) is sunk by kamikaze
Italian Republic established
Rear Admiral Sidney W. Souers, USNR, ends term as 1st director of CIA. Lieutenant General Hoyt S. Vandenberg, USA, becomes 2nd director of CIA
Saab produces its first automobile
Istvan Dobi becomes Hungarian premier
Germany doesn't annex Oder-Neissegrens
Chicago White Sox outfielder Sam Mele is only 6th MLB player to record 6 RBI's in an inning (4th) during a 15-4 win over the A's in Philadelphia
US President Harry Truman expresses a desire to nationalize the steel industry
KQED TV channel 9 in San Francisco, California (PBS) begins broadcasting
PBS reaches SF: KQED (Channel 9) starts broadcasting
First separation of a virus into component parts is reported
KWEX TV channel 41 in San Antonio, TX (IND) begins broadcasting
XVI Summer Olympic equestrian events open in Stockholm
Rocky Colovito hits 4 consecutive HRs in 1 game
A one-day record 54 home runs hit in baseball
Igor Ter-Ovanesyan of USSR, sets then long jump record at 27' 3½"
Rolling Stones record their "12x5" album at Chess Studios in Chicago, Illinois
Southern filibuster on civil rights bill ends; cloture invoked
Vietnam War: Battle of Dong Xoai begins, a major engagement between the Viet Cong and South Vietnamese forces
Cleveland Indian Sonny Siebert no-hits Washington Senator, 2-0
Mamas & Papas win gold record for "Monday, Monday"
The Beatles release single "Paperback Writer" / "Rain" in UK; "Rain" featured experimental studio tricks of slowed down bass and drums tracks, and backwards vocals in the fade out
15,000 attend Fantasy Faire and Magic Mountain Music Festival, California
American pole vaulter Bob Seagren sets a world record mark of 17'7" in San Diego, California
Argentina becomes a member of the Berne Convention copyright treaty
Israel, Syria, Jordan, Iraq & Egypt end "6-Day War" with UN help
USSR drops diplomatic relations with Israel
KCFW TV channel 9 in Kalispell, MT (ABC/NBC) begins broadcasting
WHTV (now WTZH) TV channel 24 in Meridian, MS (NBC/CBS) 1st broadcast
11 die in a train crash in Salem, Illinois
44th National Spelling Bee: Jonathan Knisely wins spelling "shalloon"
"Too Young" single released by Donny Osmond
American rock band the Grateful Dead perform their longest concert, 30 songs clocking in at approximately 4:41:37, at Robert F. Kennedy Memorial Stadium in Washington, D.C.
NASA launches Radio Astronomy Explorer 49 into lunar orbit
Rumor's government in Italy resigns
Rockefeller panel reports on 300,000 illegal CIA files on Americans
Yankees sponsor Army Day at their temporary home, Shea Stadium; during a 21-gun salute, part of the fence is blown away, and another part is set on fire
49th National Spelling Bee: Tim Kneale wins spelling narcolepsy

American golferAl Geibergerbecomes the first player in history to post a sub-60 score, 59 (−13), in a PGA Tour-sanctioned event during the second round of the Danny Thomas Memphis Classic at Colonial CC in Tennessee
Apple Computer ships its first Apple II computers
International Labour Organisation and United Nations meet to discuss apartheid in South Africa and potential actions to prevent further violence and state repression.
Costa Rica becomes a member of the Berne Convention copyright treaty
Yankees trade Ken Holzman for Ron Davis
Baltimore Orioles pull their 8th triple play (5-4-3 vs Cleveland)
Eight Provisional Irish Republican Army prisoners escape from Crumlin Road Gaol in Belfast using handguns smuggled into the prison
IRA's Joseph Doherty escapes from Crumlin Road Jail
Seven Brothers Square in Bronx named honoring 7 Santini Bro Moving Co
American TV sitcom "Taxi" last airs on ABC, cancelled after 4 seasons and 15 Emmy Award wins; moves to NBC in the fall, airing for 1 season and wins 3 more Emmys
Battle of Sultan Yakoub: Israeli raid into Syria results in 30 deaths and the capture of Israeli tanks
Iran-Iraq War: Iran recaptures Khorramshahr, Iran from Iraq
Israeli troops reach outskirts of Beirut, Lebanon
John N. McMahon replaces Bobby R. Inman becomes deputy director of CIA
US missile shoots down an incoming missile in space for first time
Zhu Jian Hua of China high jumps a record 7'10" (2.39m)
Claus von Bülow acquitted during second trial on charges he tried to murder his wife
Coca-Cola announces they're bringing back their 99-year-old formula
In South Africa, the three-year-old 'State of Emergency' is renewed for another twelve months, followed by an organized campaign of civil disobedience against it
NASA Discovery's SRBs & external tank are mated
Greatest number of participants (31,678) on a bicycle tour (London)
"Tales From The Crypt" TV Anthology debuts on HBO
Burger King begins using Newman's Own Salad Dressing
Detroit Pistons beat Trailblazers for 1st time since 1974 in Portland
Rap group "2 Live Crew" members arrested in Florida for obscenity
New York welcomes home troops from Operation Desert Storm in the Mother of All Parades
South Florida and Denver picked for 1993 NL franchises
Satellite Intelsat K launched
Biggest European clock ever (9100 kg/(237) 2.5 m) at Aarle-Rixtel
China performs nuclear test at Lop Nor, PRC
Orioles Jeff Manto, hits his 4th consecutive homer
Howard Stern Radio Show premieres in Toledo, Ohio, on WBUZ 106.5 FM
Stanley Cup Final, Miami Arena, Miami, Florida: Uwe Krupp's unassisted goal in triple-OT gives Colorado Avalanche a 1-0 win over Florida Panthers and a 4 game series sweep
Feng Yun-2B Long March 3 Launch (China) is successful
Kevin Brown of Florida Marlins no hits SF Giants 9-0
Stanley Cup Final, Reunion Arena, Dallas, TX: New Jersey Devils defeat Dallas Stars, 2-1 in double OT for a 4-2 series victory
The first direct electronic communication experiment between the nervous systems of two humans is carried out by Kevin Warwick in the United Kingdom
Michael Leshner and Michael Stark become the first same-sex couple to legally marry in Canada
"The Sopranos" series finale on HBO (infamous "cut to black" ending)
61st Tony Awards: "Spring Awakening" (musical) & "The Coast of Utopia" (play) win
Novelty dance song "I'm a Gummy Bear" by German band Gummibär released
Holy Roman EmperorFrederick IBarbarossa drowns while crossing the Saleph River in modern-day Turkey, leading an army to Jerusalem during the Third Crusade
Dutch privateerPiet Heynattacks Portuguese ships in the Bay of All Saints, Brazil
Benjamin Franklintests the lightning conductor with his kite-flying experiment
Puritan version ofShakespeare's "Othello" opens in Newport, Rhode Island
British customs officials seizeJohn Hancock's ship, "The Liberty", on the suspicion that Hancock had illegally unloaded cargo without paying duties a month earlier
British explorer CaptainJames Cookis the first European to discover the Great Barrier Reef off Australia - by running aground on its coral causing the ship to leak (ship later beached and repaired)
Opera venue Teatro Nuovo (now Teatro Rossini) opens withGioachino Rossini's "La gaza ladra" (The Thieving Magpie") in Pesaro, Italy

Andrew Jackson's African Grey parrot "Poll" is removed from his funeral for swearing at The Hermitage, Tennessee. Funeral attendee William Menefee Norment records: "Before the sermon and while the crowd was gathering, a wicked parrot that was a household pet got excited and commenced swearing so loud and long as to disturb the people and had to be carried from the house”
Battle of Brice's Crossroads, Mississippi; 3,500 troops led by ConfederateNathan Bedford Forrestdefeats a Federal force of 8000
Richard Wagner's opera "Tristan und Isolde" (Tristan and Isolde) premieres in Munich, Germany
Queen Wilhelminaopens Rembrandt house in Amsterdam
Clyde BarrowandBonnie Parkerflip their car into a ravine. Parker suffers serious third degree burns from the accident which would affect her for the rest of her life.
John Dillingerrobs his first bank, taking $10,600 from the National Bank in New Carlisle, Ohio
US PresidentFranklin D. Rooseveltissues executive order making Mount Rushmore, in the Black Hills of South Dakota, part of the National Park Service

Nazis kill all inhabitants of Lidice, Protectorate of Bohemia and Moravia (now Czech Republic) which had been implicated in the assassination ofReinhard Heydrich, Nazi controller of Bohemia and Moravia, to “teach the Czechs a final lesson of subservience and humility”; over 170 adult men were executed by firing squad on site, women and children were sent to concentration camp gas chambers, and the village was burned down and plowed under
Heinrich Himmlerorders the final liquidation of Lodz ghetto in occupied Poland
John Diefenbaker(C) elected Prime Minister of Canada
MLB Cleveland Indians'Rocky Colavitohits his 4 home runs in 11-8 win over the Orioles, in Baltimore
US Equal Pay Act signed into law by PresidentJohn F. Kennedy
Janis Joplinplays her 1st live gig with Big Brother & The Holding Company, at Avalon Ballroom in San Francisco, California
"Danny ThomasHour" last airs on NBC-TV
AL games at Baltimore & Chicago postponed honoringRobert F. Kennedy
Elvis Presleyrecords a live album at NYC's Madison Square Garden
Hank Aaron's grandslammer (14) ties him for NL lead withGil Hodges& moves him ahead ofWillie Maysas #2 HR hitter (649)
Sammy Davis Jr.'s cover of "The Candy Man" by Anthony Newley and Leslie Bricusse (from the film "Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory") goes to #1 on the charts - his biggest single

Philadelphia Phillies sluggerMike Schmidthits a ball off public address speaker hanging from Houston Astrodome roof
67,000 fans attendsPaul McCartney& Wings concert at the Kingdome in Seattle, Washington
James Earl Ray(Martin Luther King's killer) escapes from prison

Sebastian Coeof England sets 800m record (1:41.73) in Florence

Intelreleases 200 MHz Pentium chip
Variety talk show "TheRosie O'DonnellShow" hosted by Rosie O'Donnell premieres
Khmer Rouge leaderPol Potorders the killing of his defense chief Son Sen and 11 of Sen's family members before Pol Pot flees his northern stronghold
NATO suspends its air strikes afterSlobodan Miloševićagrees to withdraw Serbian forces from Kosovo during the Kosovo War
PopeJohn Paul IIcanonizes Lebanon's first female saint, Saint Rafqa
Stephen Schwartz' musical "Wicked", starringIdina Menzeland Kristin Chenoweth, officially opens at the Curran Theatre in San Francisco, California
Former chief minister to King Henry VIII,Thomas Cromwellis arrested in Westminster for treason and heresy
First victim of the Salem witch trials, Bridget Bishop, is hanged after being found guilty of witchcraft in the Colony of Massachusetts
US Marines land in Cuba during the Spanish–American War
In Akron, Ohio, Dr. Robert H. Smith (Dr. Bob) from Akron & Bill Wilson from New York City form Alcoholics Anonymous (date of Smith's last drink)
The Spirit Rover is launched, beginning NASA's Mars Exploration Rover mission
